findings.evidence: The file proves only forward lemmas at lines 119, 129, and 140; the nearby analogous API uses an iff for equivalence transport: `code/TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/Transport.lean:127: lemma isComplexLinearMap_arrowCongr_iff ... ↔ IsComplexLinearMap J J' F`.
findings.fix: Add the natural iff lemmas, e.g. `(ω.transport e).Invariant (J.transport e) ↔ ω.Invariant J`, `(ω.transport e).Tames (J.transport e) ↔ ω.Tames J`, and `(ω.transport e).Compatible (J.transport e) ↔ ω.Compatible J`, then derive the forward methods from them.
findings.issue: `Invariant.transport`, `Tames.transport`, and `Compatible.transport` only prove the forward direction along a linear equivalence, even though transport by `e.symm` gives reflection for free.

summary: The transport construction is at the right object level, but the predicate API is under-generalized: equivalence transport should preserve and reflect invariance, tameness, and compatibility.
